POL-374.title: IMPLEMENT: Harden Polaris distribution readiness and tracker lifecycle semantics
POL-374.body:
## Objective

Make Polaris safe and useful for clean new users and new projects by hardening distribution metadata, onboarding, artifact hygiene, tracker lifecycle policy, tracker adapter boundaries, and external-repo proof.

## Context

Created from analysis issue [POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle). The analysis artifact is `smartdocs/raw/pol-373-distribution-readiness-tracker-lifecycle-analysis.md` and should be ingested/promoted through SmartDocs rather than written directly to active specs.

Polaris already has substantial adoption support in `polaris init` and `polaris init --adopt`, but distribution-readiness is not proven. The current repository still has sparse README/package metadata, no `polaris config doctor` command, fragmented artifact hygiene, and Linear-centric tracker lifecycle behavior.

## Goal

Deliver an implementation cluster that makes the clean new-user flow auditable: install or local package use, adopt an external repo, validate configuration, avoid runtime artifact bleed, and apply tracker lifecycle transitions through a tracker-agnostic policy surface.

## Scope

* package.json
* README.md
* src/cli/\*\*
* src/config/\*\*
* src/finalize/\*\*
* src/tracker/\*\*
* src/loop/\*\*
* test/\*\*
* smartdocs/raw/[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle)-distribution-readiness-tracker-lifecycle-analysis.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Package metadata and README describe a credible local/global install and first-run path.
- [ ] `polaris config doctor` or equivalent readiness check reports actionable bootstrap/provider/tracker/artifact status.
- [ ] Runtime artifact hygiene is enforced consistently for adoption and finalize.
- [ ] Tracker lifecycle policy is represented in config, validated, and mapped through adapter capabilities.
- [ ] Dispatch, validation, triage, and finalize lifecycle transitions are policy-backed or skipped with explicit reasons.
- [ ] A fresh external-repo fixture proves adoption/run artifact hygiene and lifecycle behavior.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli src/config src/tracker src/loop src/finalize test

## Ordering

Run children in order. Later children depend on the architecture/policy surfaces introduced by earlier children.

## Non-goals

* Do not publish the package to npm.
* Do not implement full Jira or GitHub tracker adapters beyond interface/capability scaffolding unless explicitly scoped by a child.
* Do not mark Linear issues Done/Closed automatically unless a child explicitly changes the review-gated policy with tests.

POL-376.title: IMPLEMENT: Unify artifact hygiene policy across adoption and finalize
POL-376.body:
## Objective

Make runtime/adoption/canonical artifact classification consistent across init/adoption, finalize, gitignore generation, and staging enforcement.

## Context

[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle) found two partial artifact policies: adoption appends broad runtime exclusions and unstages selected paths, while finalize has a richer classifier in `src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts`. Recent delivery history still shows run-specific state and runtime artifacts appearing around commits. This child depends on the distribution/config-doctor surface so doctor can report the unified policy.

## Goal

Create one shared artifact hygiene policy used by adoption and finalize, with tests proving commit-eligible artifacts and runtime scratch are classified consistently.

## Scope

* src/finalize/artifact-policy.ts
* src/finalize/\*\*
* src/cli/init.ts
* src/cli/\*\*
* src/config/\*\*
* test/\*\*
* .gitignore

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Adoption and finalize use the same classifier or a shared source of truth for Polaris-managed artifact paths.
- [ ] Runtime scratch, current-state, run reports, bootstrap packets, mutation queues, and foreign cluster artifacts are never staged by default.
- [ ] Commit-eligible adoption outputs and durable active-cluster evidence remain stageable when policy allows them.
- [ ] `.gitignore` generation covers the policy's active runtime/crash-recovery classes.
- [ ] Tests cover path classification and adoption/finalize staging behavior.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/finalize src/cli test

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-375](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-375/implement-add-distribution-metadata-readme-quickstart-and-config).

## Non-goals

* Do not change tracker lifecycle semantics.
* Do not remove existing durable cluster evidence from repository history.
* Do not publish or package the CLI.

POL-377.title: IMPLEMENT: Add tracker lifecycle policy schema and resolver
POL-377.body:
## Objective

Represent tracker lifecycle semantics in Polaris config as normalized policy rather than hardcoded Linear state names.

## Context

[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle) identified that child dispatch, validation-passed, merged, triage, parent completion, and provider-failure tracker behavior need tracker-agnostic lifecycle semantics. Current config has tracker adapter fields but no lifecycle policy schema or resolver.

## Goal

Add a validated `tracker.lifecyclePolicy` config surface with safe defaults, normalized lifecycle states, and a resolver that downstream runtime/tracker code can call.

## Scope

* src/config/schema.ts
* src/config/schema.json
* src/config/defaults.ts
* src/config/validator.ts
* src/tracker/\*\*
* test/\*\*

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config types and JSON schema include `tracker.lifecyclePolicy` with normalized transition keys.
- [ ] Defaults preserve current review-gated behavior and avoid false implementation-failure status changes on provider/runtime failures before repo work.
- [ ] Validation rejects malformed lifecycle policy values with actionable errors.
- [ ] A resolver returns transition intent, skip behavior, and evidence requirements for child dispatch, validation passed, merged, triage required, parent all-children-complete, parent delivery merged, and provider failure before work.
- [ ] Tests cover defaults, valid overrides, invalid values, and missing tracker configuration.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/config src/tracker

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-376](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-376/implement-unify-artifact-hygiene-policy-across-adoption-and-finalize).

## Non-goals

* Do not apply tracker mutations in runtime yet.
* Do not implement GitHub or Jira adapters.
* Do not change Linear finalize review-gate behavior except to express it in policy defaults.

POL-378.title: IMPLEMENT: Define tracker adapter capability contract and native mappings
POL-378.body:
## Objective

Define the tracker adapter boundary Polaris needs for Linear, GitHub Issues, Jira, and local file-backed tasks without implementing full new remote adapters.

## Context

[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle) found that current tracker support is Linear-centric: Linear sync-in produces an execution graph, MCP bridge mutation support is placeholder-shaped and Linear-specific, and `LinearAdapter.syncOut()` is not implemented. Tracker lifecycle policy needs adapter capability metadata and native status mapping.

## Goal

Introduce a common tracker task/capability contract and implement capability/status mapping for existing Linear and local-file surfaces, with stubs or documented capability descriptors for GitHub Issues and Jira.

## Scope

* src/tracker/\*\*
* src/config/\*\*
* test/tracker/\*\*
* test/\*\*
* smartdocs/raw/[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle)-distribution-readiness-tracker-lifecycle-analysis.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Tracker adapters expose capabilities for child relationships, status updates, comments, links, dependencies, and lifecycle-state mapping.
- [ ] Linear maps native workflow states to normalized lifecycle states without hardcoding a single team's state names.
- [ ] Local-file tracker support has a stable file-backed task model or explicit capability descriptor.
- [ ] GitHub Issues and Jira are represented by capability/interface documentation or non-runtime descriptors sufficient for future implementation planning.
- [ ] Unsupported transitions return explicit skip/unsupported results with reasons.
- [ ] Tests cover capability descriptors and status mapping behavior.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/tracker test/tracker

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-377](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-377/implement-add-tracker-lifecycle-policy-schema-and-resolver).

## Non-goals

* Do not build complete GitHub Issues or Jira adapters.
* Do not perform live tracker mutations.
* Do not change worker dispatch behavior yet.

POL-379.title: IMPLEMENT: Apply tracker lifecycle policy in loop and finalize runtime boundaries
POL-379.body:
## Objective

Apply policy-backed tracker lifecycle transitions at dispatch, validation, triage, and finalize boundaries without falsely marking provider/runtime failures as implementation failures.

## Context

[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle) found that tracker status changes are currently incomplete and Linear-centric. `TrackerSyncService` queues validated done children as `state: Done`, finalize moves the parent toward review, and child dispatch/failure/triage transitions are not driven by a normalized lifecycle policy.

## Goal

Integrate the lifecycle policy and tracker capability contract into the loop/finalize runtime so transitions are idempotent, evidence-backed, and skipped with explicit reasons when unsupported.

## Scope

* src/loop/\*\*
* src/finalize/\*\*
* src/tracker/\*\*
* src/cluster-state/\*\*
* test/loop/\*\*
* test/tracker/\*\*
* test/\*\*

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Child dispatch can request the configured active/in-progress lifecycle transition when the tracker supports it.
- [ ] Validation-passed child completion queues or applies the configured review/done lifecycle transition with completion evidence.
- [ ] Failed validation or triage-required worker results map to the configured blocked/triage lifecycle transition.
- [ ] Provider/runtime failure before repo work records no implementation failure status unless policy explicitly asks for one.
- [ ] Parent all-children-complete and delivery-merged transitions are policy-backed and keep the existing Done/Closed review gate safe by default.
- [ ] Telemetry or cluster-state records transition attempts, skips, conflicts, and failures.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/loop src/finalize src/tracker test/loop test/tracker

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-378](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-378/implement-define-tracker-adapter-capability-contract-and-native).

## Non-goals

* Do not implement full GitHub/Jira adapters.
* Do not bypass Linear review-gate protections.
* Do not change worker implementation scope parsing.

POL-380.title: IMPLEMENT: Add fresh external-repo adoption and lifecycle proof
POL-380.body:
## Objective

Prove Polaris can be adopted and run in a clean external repository without relying on Polaris-repo-specific state or leaking runtime artifacts.

## Context

[POL-373](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-373/analyze-distribution-readiness-gaps-and-tracker-adapter-lifecycle) identified provider-independent first-run proof as the acceptance test for distribution readiness. Earlier children add package/onboarding, config doctor, artifact policy, lifecycle policy, adapter capabilities, and runtime integration. This child validates those pieces together in a small fixture or synthetic repo.

## Goal

Add an automated fresh-repo fixture test or smoke test that exercises install/local package use, `polaris init --adopt`, `polaris config doctor`, a small local or tracker-backed run, lifecycle transition behavior, and git artifact hygiene.

## Scope

* test/\*\*
* src/cli/\*\*
* src/config/\*\*
* src/tracker/\*\*
* src/loop/\*\*
* src/finalize/\*\*
* package.json
* README.md

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test creates or uses a clean fixture repo outside the Polaris repository tree.
- [ ] Test runs the local built/packed Polaris CLI or equivalent local install path.
- [ ] `polaris init --adopt` succeeds or dry-run plus approved path is covered deterministically.
- [ ] `polaris config doctor` reports readiness without assuming the Polaris repository's local tracker/provider stack.
- [ ] A local or tracker-backed parent task can produce/run at least one child or deterministic stub worker result.
- [ ] Lifecycle transitions are applied or skipped with explicit reasons according to adapter capabilities.
- [ ] `git status --short` in the fixture does not show runtime scratch, current-state, bootstrap packets, mutation queues, or foreign cluster artifacts staged for delivery.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run test src/cli src/config src/tracker src/loop src/finalize

## Ordering

* Depends on [POL-379](https://linear.app/lsctech/issue/POL-379/implement-apply-tracker-lifecycle-policy-in-loop-and-finalize-runtime).

## Non-goals

* Do not publish to npm.
* Do not require real Linear/GitHub/Jira credentials for the fixture.
* Do not depend on this repository's current `.polaris` runtime state.
